questions.q: Describe the different types of shares that can be issued by a company and their characteristics. Explain the advantages and disadvantages of each type.
questions.answer: There are two main types of shares: Equity Shares and Preference Shares.
marking_scheme: Explain the characteristics of Equity Shares (1 mark)
Explain the characteristics of Preference Shares (1 mark)
Explain the advantages and disadvantages of Equity Shares (1 mark)
Explain the advantages and disadvantages of Preference Shares (2 marks)

questions.q: Explain the process of issuing shares and the steps involved in it. What are the different methods of issuing shares?
questions.answer: The process of issuing shares involves the following steps: Determination of Issue Price, Preparation of Prospectus, Filing of Documents, and Allotment of Shares.
marking_scheme: Explain the steps involved in issuing shares (2 marks)
Explain the different methods of issuing shares (3 marks)

questions.q: What is the difference between equity share capital and preference share capital? Explain the accounting treatment for each type of share capital.
questions.answer: Equity share capital is the share capital that is issued to the shareholders and preference share capital is the share capital that has a fixed return.
marking_scheme: Explain the difference between equity share capital and preference share capital (2 marks)
Explain the accounting treatment for equity share capital (2 marks)
Explain the accounting treatment for preference share capital (1 mark)

questions.q: Calculate the amount of share capital to be transferred to the general reserve when ₹ 1,00,000 is transferred from the profit and loss account to the general reserve.
questions.answer:
Calculation: 
Share Capital to be transferred = ₹ 1,00,000
Total Share Capital = ₹ 1,00,000
Proportion of General Reserve = ₹ 1,00,000 / ₹ 1,00,000 = 1
marking_scheme: Calculate the proportion of general reserve (2 marks)
Calculate the amount of share capital to be transferred (3 marks)

questions.q: A company issues 10,000 shares of ₹ 10 each at a premium of ₹ 2 per share. Calculate the total amount of share capital and the amount of share premium received.
questions.answer:
Calculation: 
Total Amount of Share Capital = Number of Shares × Face Value of Each Share
= 10,000 × ₹ 10
= ₹ 1,00,000
Amount of Share Premium = Number of Shares × Premium per Share
= 10,000 × ₹ 2
= ₹ 20,000
marking_scheme: Calculate the total amount of share capital (3 marks)
Calculate the amount of share premium received (2 marks)

questions.q: A company issues 5,000 shares of ₹ 100 each at a discount of ₹ 10 per share. Calculate the amount of share premium to be transferred to the capital redemption reserve.
questions.answer:
Calculation: 
Number of Shares Issued = 5,000
Face Value of Each Share = ₹ 100
Discount per Share = ₹ 10
Total Amount of Share Capital = Number of Shares × Face Value of Each Share
= 5,000 × ₹ 100
= ₹ 5,00,000
Total Discount = Number of Shares × Discount per Share
= 5,000 × ₹ 10
= ₹ 50,000
Amount of Share Premium to be Transferred = Total Discount
= ₹ 50,000
marking_scheme: Calculate the total amount of share capital (3 marks)
Calculate the amount of share premium to be transferred (2 marks)
